question: Broad knowledge of genetic and genomic analytical and testing methodologies
Critical evaluation of scientific and medical literature
Interpretation and use of major genetic and genomic databases (e.g., OMIM, gnomAD, ClinVar, COSMIC)
‘Omics-relevant statistical analysis
Validating informatics software
Interpreting results / data in either a germline or somatic context
Following protocols, standard operating procedures (SOPs) and guidelines
Establishing frameworks to collect, analyze, and interpret data and information from a variety of sources
Tolerance / acceptance of uncertainty and ambiguity
Tolerance for repetitive / inconclusive, and for troubleshooting inconclusive / failed work
Maintaining good records and documentation of dry lab work / clinical work (e.g., spreadsheets, reports)
Writing clear, tactful, and professional emails
Generating and using slides effectively (e.g., PowerPoint, Keynote)
Presenting to / teaching scientists or medical professionals
Effectively and succinctly synthesizing / summarizing complex information to generate insights
Training and mentoring individuals
Seeking advice and asking for feedback / clarification from advisors and mentors when needed
Demonstrating workplace etiquette (e.g., punctuality, consideration, flexibility, gratitude)
Complying with rules, guidelines, policies, laws, and regulations
Upholding commitments and meeting deadlines
Working effectively in a hierarchical team (e.g., collaboration, sharing timely updates, willingness to take on tasks for the good of the project / team, reporting to a team lead / supervisor)
Managing / leading a team, and / or working with direct reports
Planning, organizing, establishing priorities, and time management
Adapting to changing goals, projects, and circumstances at work
Creativity / innovative and strategic thinking
Problem solving and critical thinking
Working under pressure / managing tight deadlines
Resilience, self-awareness, and mindfulness of personal wellness and well-being
Building and maintaining positive relationships with colleagues, and an encouraging, supportive, and psychologically safe workplace culture
Listening / being attentive
Providing constructive feedback
Contributing to and incorporating awareness and adoption of inclusion, diversity, equity, and accessibility issues and guidelines in the workplace
Identifying career options and setting career goals
Creating and maintaining a professional network (e.g., meeting people, maintaining connections)

Marketing oneself effectively when seeking and applying for job opportunities (e.g., building a strong professional reputation, pitching oneself to potential employers, standing out from other applicants)
Understanding privacy and disclosure issues in the context of medical genetics
Understanding responsible conduct in human research (e.g., clinical trials, obtaining ethics approval)
Demonstrating responsible writing practices (i.e., referencing appropriately)
Demonstrating accountability for one’s actions, and a dedication to acting in accordance with the highest standards of professionalism
Demonstrating cultural understanding and humility
Writing new code, or optimizing / heavily modifying existing code
Installing, testing, and debugging bioinformatics software for ‘omics data analysis
Variant filtering, prioritization, and triaging from ‘omics data
Performing literature searches and reviews
Critically reading scientific and medical literature
Using literature and / or new data to draw conclusions and make hypotheses
Using & interpreting databases of genetic and genomic information
Working with genome browsers
Interpreting information on existing published variants
Interpreting information on novel variants
Solving research / clinical mysteries and answering important scientific questions about human health and disease
Lifelong learning and continuous professional development
Working directly with patients
Recruiting and tracking patients in clinical trials, including patient consenting and data collection
Communicating with / working with the media
Teaching in a classroom setting
Community service / social responsibility
Participating on workplace / organizational committees
Networking / making authentic connections with others in a professional setting
Work-related travel
Attending conferences or scientific meetings
Analyzing and managing financial data or budgets
